title: The Macro Minute | January 24, 2022

good morning everyone darius still here
to present our macro minute for monday
january 24th 2022 starting with markets
we have stocks down with europe
significantly underperforming the us and
asia commodities down crypto down dollar
up global bond yields down big movers
this morning we have germany's tax index
and copper down two percent bitcoin off
five percent ethereum down eight percent
and the bloomberg galaxy crypto index
which is a broader measure of
cryptocurrencies crashing down 16 this
morning uh bloomberg was out with an
article this morning saying um digital
coins are cryptocurrencies have shed
more than one trillion of a value of
market cap since their november highs if
you recall this rough our analysis
suggests there's roughly about 2.6
trillion in excess savings uh on the
consumer balance sheet at least here in
the us and so to sucking a trillion
dollars out of that is not exactly uh uh
part of the bull case so moving on to
the economic front uh the preliminary
january pmi data confirmed omicron did
in fact depress output that said the
impact doesn't appear to be as pervasive
as what we observed last summer with
delta that means global supply chain
disruptions should continue to be
unwound here in q1 without much if any
delay uh so improving we had japan
manufacturing germany manufacturing
germany services and eurozone
manufacturing uh declining we had
australia manufacturing
australia services japan services france
manufacturing eurozone services uk
manufacturing and uk services uspmi the
market pmi data will be out later this
morning a busy week of macro and micro
updates in general with the fomc meeting
on wednesday is probably the biggest
event of the week we're going to be
watching for clues on the timing and
pace of qt in terms of that program and
whether a 50 basis point rate height
rate hike in march is a legitimate
possibility how assets trade into the
close wednesday and on thursday will go
a long way to determining how risk ads
or risk assets are likely to trade over
the next couple of months um q4 gdp data
out thursday uh consumer income and
spending data uh friday we will get the
core pc data point that's the fed's
preferred inflation metric again that
number's on friday and then lastly you
know we don't spend a ton of time
talking about things that uh you can't
model with numbers uh but the situation
in ukraine bears watching uh after dc
after the the the powers that be down in
dc ordered family members of its embassy
staff in kiev to leave due to the threat
of russian military action our take on
this and our take on every other
international conflict is that it
doesn't pay to guess uh when it comes to
geopolitical risk you're better off
building and paying or and or paying for
if you can't build it yourself
uh for a set of repeatable and and
robust and reliable risk management
tools that give you clean buy the dip or
get the heck out signals and so that's
obviously what we sell here at 42 maco
so come check us out if you don't have
those already with respect to today's
probable range setups over about silver
and six silver oversold signal in
germany's dax s p 500 bitcoin ethereum
that bloomberg galaxy crypto index the
british pound and the us dollar relative
to the brazilian real s p 500 is neutral
van still it's at the lower boundary of
its probable range with nine point one
percent upside uh wti bullish fam steel
with six percent
uh downside versus five percent of
upside bloomberg dollar index neutral
van still minus one point one percent of
downside versus zero point seven percent
of upside ten year treasury yield
bullish fam still minus four basis
points of downside versus 22 basis
points of upside bitcoin taking it to
the chart here is that the lower
boundary of its probable range with 27
percent of upside i mean i do believe we
think we sold our bitcoin somewhere
around there on december 10th and uh you
know didn't want to sell it i mentioned
that um you know our fundamental
longer-term view on bitcoin and ethereum
and cryptocurrency in general has not
changed but what did change is our risk
management signals told us to get the
heck out and so that's exactly what we
did and that was a good call um you know
but right now it's at the lower boundary
problem range so um if you're a
longer-term cryptocurrency investor or
someone who hasn't you know participated
in the asset class this would be the
spot to to take a shot you know last
time we saw the lower boundary the
probable range we had a little bit of a
lift and then the previous time before
that we had a much bigger lift so you
know this would be the time to take the
shot but again you know if
things continue to get hairy in this
asset class obviously it tends to have a
life of its own when it's going to these
bear markets so and that's it that is
what it is uh ethereum at the lower
boundary's proper range with 46 percent
of upside so probably a better buy if
you're going to buy anything in that
space and then lastly gold neutral vans
with 2.9 percent downside versus 0.8
percent of upside so with that dairy
